mission: Make Stripe the default programmable financial infrastructure layer for internet businesses by improving payment conversion, recurring revenue automation, platform money movement, and operational trust in one system.

businessModel: Transaction-based revenue from payments and money movement
Software revenue from billing, tax, revenue recognition, and finance automation products
Platform and marketplace revenue from Connect, payouts, and embedded financial workflows
Risk and value-added service revenue from fraud, identity, and adjacent financial products
Expansion economics driven by higher product depth per customer and durable GPV retention
marketForces: Merchants now expect payment providers to improve conversion and authorization, not only process transactions
Software and AI-native businesses increasingly need recurring, usage-based, and global monetization infrastructure out of the box
Platforms want embedded fintech capabilities without building bespoke compliance and ledger infrastructure
Fraud, identity assurance, and compliance controls must preserve growth rather than act as blunt blockers
Finance and operations leaders increasingly evaluate payment infrastructure on reporting, reconciliation, and close reliability
keyPartners: Card networks, issuers, and acquiring partners
Local payment-method providers and banking rails
Merchants, software companies, and platforms building on Stripe
Tax, identity, and compliance data providers
Reader hardware, treasury, and financial institution partners

products.name: Trust, Identity, and Finance Operations Foundations

designPrinciples: Keep checkout, payment-method orchestration, and authorization optimization close enough that conversion tradeoffs remain visible.
Treat billing, tax, revenue recognition, and finance reporting as one revenue-automation system rather than isolated modules.
Keep Connect onboarding, funds routing, and payouts tightly coupled because operator trust depends on the full money-movement loop.
Preserve strong shared ledger, risk, identity, and eventing foundations because every Stripe product depends on money-state correctness.
Use shared reporting and dashboard foundations so operators can understand payments, billing, and platform workflows without stitching separate systems together.

recommendedStructure.mission: Own checkout conversion, payment methods, and authorization performance.
Own subscriptions, usage billing, invoicing, tax, and accounting automation.
Own connected-account lifecycle, platform funds flows, and payouts.
Own fraud, disputes, ledgering, reconciliation, and shared operator trust.
Own APIs, dashboard foundations, webhooks, data products, and shared developer experience.

operatingModelNotes: Stripe compounds when a merchant or platform can adopt more of the stack without re-architecting its internal operations.
The central product challenge is balancing programmable flexibility with operator legibility and financial correctness.
Money-state correctness is a shared-platform problem; it should not fragment across payments, billing, and connect teams.
